plan_metadata.title: EAFIX Work-Cell Decomposition — Phase 1 & 2 Execution Plan
plan_metadata.tier_rationale: Phases 1 and 2 produce documentation and derived catalog artifacts, not executable code. Full pattern-registry machinery (PATTERN_INDEX.yaml, EXEC-* executors) does not yet exist. Template sections borrowed: referenced_context_artifacts, artifact_intent_manifest, step_contracts, anti_pattern_detection, validation_gates.

phases_deferred.PH-03: Source refactoring — apply full newPhasePlanProcess v3.3 template when pattern registry is bootstrapped.
phases_deferred.PH-04: AI routing rules — apply full newPhasePlanProcess v3.3 template.

critical_constraint.name: NO_IMPLIED_BEHAVIOR
rules: Every intended file mutation MUST be declared in artifact_intent_manifest before execution begins.
No AUTHORITATIVE context artifact may be modified during Phases 1 or 2.
Every work cell entry in the catalog MUST reference at least one required_reference_document.
Every work cell MUST declare forbidden_dependencies that prevent cross-boundary contamination.
Every work cell MUST have its parent_module resolved to a canonical module in module_catalog.json.
Context packets MUST NOT override or contradict canonical source documents.
Steps that cannot be mechanically validated are marked NOT_AUTOMATABLE with manual evidence required.
Staleness policy: if any AUTHORITATIVE artifact changes after this plan is created, the catalog entries for affected modules must be re-derived before Phases 3 or 4 begin.

policy.note: Every step that touches a work cell or context packet MUST cite the applicable context_artifact_ids from this list. A step MAY NOT rely on unstated external context.

artifacts.purpose: Canonical registry of all EAFIX modules. Work cell parent_module values must resolve here.
Canonical process step definitions. Work cells within process-step boundaries must be consistent with these definitions.
Aligned trading process specification. Phase and lifecycle sequencing must be consistent with this document.
Canonical transport and channel definitions. MT4 bridge work cells (B1-B3) must derive allowed/forbidden dependencies from this document.
Canonical UI screen and gateway definitions. UI work cells must enumerate screens and endpoints from this document only.
Deterministic calendar rules. Calendar work cells (D2, D3, F2, D4) must list this as a required_reference_document and must not contradict its rules.
MT4 platform constraints and OrderSend semantics. B1-B3 work cells must treat this as the binding MT4 reference.
Canonical mapping of source files to modules. Context packet allowed_files lists must be subsets of this mapping for the target module.
Services reference. Work cells that cross service boundaries must verify those boundaries against this document.
Routing instructions for AI context. Work cell required_reference_documents lists must be consistent with this document's per-module routing rules.

Parent decomposition document. The submodule_context_catalog.json is a derived artifact of this document and must not contradict it. However, this plan may extend it with enforcement details not present in the original.

assumptions: All AUTHORITATIVE context artifacts listed above exist on disk and are readable before Phase 1 begins.
No pattern registry (PATTERN_INDEX.yaml) exists yet — steps are executed manually or by AI with NOT_AUTOMATABLE designation.
submodule_context_catalog.json does not yet exist; Phase 1 creates it.
Context packets do not yet exist; Phase 2 creates them.
The decomposition_model.json work cell definitions are the starting point but may be refined as canonical sources are consulted.
The eafix-modular working directory is the output location for all artifacts.
in_scope: Authoring submodule_context_catalog.json with entries for all work cells in all modules.
Validating every work cell against canonical module and process step catalogs.
Generating per-module and per-work-cell context packets.
Writing AI-specific READMEs for each context packet.
Schema validation of all produced artifacts.
out_of_scope: Bootstrapping the pattern registry — that is a prerequisite for Phase 3.
Creating new runtime services or execution machinery.

phase_1: Every work cell has parent_module resolving to CA-001 (module_catalog.json).
Every work cell lists at least one required_reference_document from the context artifacts above.
GATE-CELL-001 passes (all work cells present and complete).
GATE-NOOVERRIDE-001 passes (no AUTHORITATIVE artifact was modified).
phase_2: One context packet directory exists per canonical module.
Each packet contains: source_files list, contracts, test_files list, allowed_files list, acceptance_gates list, and ai_readme.md.
GATE-PKT-001 passes (all allowed_files are subsets of file_module_mapping.csv for that module).
GATE-STALE-001 passes (no context packet references a source file not in file_module_mapping.csv).

artifact_intent_manifest.description: Pre-execution declaration of all files that will be created or modified during Phases 1 and 2. No file mutation may occur that is not declared here.

records.purpose: Derived AI context routing layer. Contains one entry per work cell across all canonical modules.
records.path_abstraction_reason: Single flat file in working directory; not a governed cross-service boundary in Phases 1-2.

records.purpose: JSON Schema defining the required fields for every work cell entry in submodule_context_catalog.json.
records.path_abstraction_reason: Schema artifact local to working directory.

records.purpose: Root directory for all module context packets. Each subdirectory is one canonical module.
records.path_abstraction_reason: Output directory local to working directory.

records.purpose: Per-module context packet: source_files, contracts, test_files, allowed_files, acceptance_gates, forbidden_files, required_reference_documents.
records.path_abstraction_reason: Per-module output artifacts; template path uses module_id placeholder.

records.purpose: Human/AI-readable README for each module context packet explaining its purpose, required documents, allowed files, and acceptance gates.
records.path_abstraction_reason: Documentation artifact local to context packet directory.

records.purpose: Per-work-cell context file: allowed_files, forbidden_files, required_reference_documents, acceptance_gates. Subset of the parent module context packet scoped to this work cell.
JSON Schema defining the required fields for every context packet and work cell context file.
records.path_abstraction_reason: Schema artifact local to working directory.

phases.name: Derived Context Catalog
phases.description: Create submodule_context_catalog.json as the AI context routing layer. Enumerate all work cells from all canonical modules. Validate every entry against canonical sources.

phases.name: Module Context Packets
phases.description: For every canonical module, generate a context packet containing source files, contracts, tests, allowed/forbidden file lists, acceptance gates, and an AI-specific README. Each work cell within the module gets its own context file.

STEP-001.purpose: Verify all AUTHORITATIVE and CONSTRAINING context artifacts are present on disk and readable before any catalog work begins.
STEP-001.not_automatable_reason: Requires human or AI to navigate the actual filesystem and confirm each canonical source is accessible. No pattern or executor exists yet.

content_requirements: Lists each CA-* artifact
Records: path, exists (bool), readable (bool), sha256

preconditions: Working directory is eafix-modular or its equivalent path.
Executor has read access to all listed artifact paths.
postconditions: All 11 context artifacts confirmed present and readable.
Verification log written to .state/evidence/PH-01/STEP-001/.

failure_modes.meaning: One or more AUTHORITATIVE context artifacts not found on disk.
failure_modes.remediation: Locate the missing file and update the working directory or file path. Do NOT proceed until all artifacts are present.

failure_modes.meaning: Artifact exists but cannot be parsed (corrupted, binary mismatch, etc.).
failure_modes.remediation: Restore from backup. Do NOT proceed with a corrupted source.

rollback_strategy.note: No catalog changes were made; rollback is trivial.

idempotency.enforcement: Re-running overwrites the verification log with fresh results.

STEP-002.purpose: Design and write submodule_catalog_schema.json — the JSON Schema that every work cell entry in the catalog must satisfy. Gates depend on this schema.
STEP-002.not_automatable_reason: Schema design requires human judgment about required fields. Produces AINT-002.

content_requirements: Requires field: work_cell_id (string, pattern ^[A-Z][A-Z0-9]*\.\d+_.+$)
Requires field: parent_module (string, non-empty)
Requires field: purpose (string, non-empty)
Requires field: input_contracts (array, min 0)
Requires field: output_contracts (array, min 0)
Requires field: source_files (array, min 0)
Requires field: test_files (array, min 0)
Requires field: allowed_dependencies (array)
Requires field: forbidden_dependencies (array)
Requires field: required_reference_documents (array, min 1)
Requires field: validation_gates (array, min 1)
No additional properties allowed unless explicitly permitted

invariants: Schema must be stricter than the example in decomposition_model.json, not looser.
postconditions: submodule_catalog_schema.json validates as valid JSON Schema draft-07 or later.
Schema requires all fields listed in content_requirements.

failure_modes.meaning: Produced schema is not valid JSON Schema.
failure_modes.remediation: Correct the schema and re-validate with a JSON Schema validator.

failure_modes.meaning: Schema does not require all mandatory fields.
failure_modes.remediation: Add missing required constraints. GATE-SCHEMA-001 will catch this at validation time.

STEP-003.purpose: Author Calendar Pipeline work cells (D2_CALENDAR_SOURCE_ADAPTER, D3_CALENDAR_NORMALIZER, F2_EVENT_LOG, D4_CALENDAR_TRIGGER_BUILDER) into submodule_context_catalog.json. Each work cell must be authored by consulting CA-001, CA-002, CA-006, CA-009, and CA-011.

content_requirements: Entries for: D2.1_SOURCE_REGISTRY, D2.2_FEED_POLLING, D2.3_PAYLOAD_PARSING, D2.4_ERROR_CLASSIFICATION
Entries for: D3.1_TIMEZONE_NORMALIZATION, D3.2_CALENDAR_IMPACT_NORMALIZATION, D3.3_DEDUPLICATION, D3.4_IDENTIFIER_ASSIGNMENT
Entries for: F2.1_APPEND_WRITER, F2.2_IDEMPOTENCY_GUARD, F2.3_EVENT_STREAM_PUBLISH
Entries for: D4.1_ANTICIPATION_WINDOW, D4.2_SYMBOL_IMPACT_RESOLVE, D4.3_TTL_FRESHNESS_VALIDATE, D4.4_SUPPRESSION_METADATA, D4.5_TRIGGER_EMIT
All entries pass submodule_catalog_schema.json validation
All entries set forbidden_dependencies to exclude MT4 and EA namespaces
All D3.* and D4.* entries list 'EAFIX CALENDAR DES RULES.pdf' in required_reference_documents

invariants: Calendar work cells MUST NOT reference MT4 or EA files in allowed_dependencies.
postconditions: All 15 calendar work cell entries present in catalog.
python -m jsonschema -i submodule_context_catalog.json submodule_catalog_schema.json exits 0 for all added entries.

failure_modes.meaning: A work cell entry fails schema validation.
failure_modes.remediation: Correct the entry fields. All required fields must be present and non-empty.

failure_modes.meaning: parent_module value does not match any module in module_catalog.json.
failure_modes.remediation: Check module_catalog.json and correct the parent_module value.

failure_modes.meaning: A calendar work cell does not declare forbidden_dependencies excluding MT4/EA.
failure_modes.remediation: Add MT4 and EA to forbidden_dependencies for all calendar work cells.

STEP-004.purpose: Author MT4 Bridge work cells (B1_MT4_ADAPTER_TRANSPORT, B2_MT4_EA_EXECUTOR, B3_EXECUTION_EVENT_NORMALIZER) by consulting CA-004, CA-007, CA-009, CA-011.

content_requirements: Entries for: B1.1_ENVELOPE_SERIALIZATION, B1.2_ATOMIC_CSV_WRITE, B1.3_DIR_WATCHER, B1.4_SOCKET_FALLBACK
Entries for: B2.1_EXECUTION_REQUEST_READ, B2.2_PREFLIGHT_CHECK, B2.3_ORDERSEND_WRAP, B2.4_ERROR_HANDLING, B2.5_RETRY_ABORT
Entries for: B3.1_FEEDBACK_CSV_PARSE, B3.2_BROKER_EVENT_DEDUP, B3.3_RESULT_MAPPING
All B2.* entries list mt4_authoritative_reference_for_ai.json in required_reference_documents
All B1.* entries list communication_channels.json in required_reference_documents

postconditions: All 12 MT4 bridge work cell entries present in catalog.
Schema validation passes for all added entries.

failure_modes.meaning: B2.* work cell does not list mt4_authoritative_reference_for_ai.json as required_reference_document.
failure_modes.remediation: Add the reference. This is mandatory for any work cell that wraps OrderSend.

STEP-005.purpose: Author EA-Side MQL4 work cells (System A Signal Pipeline, System B Communication Bridges, System D Execution & Feedback).

content_requirements: Entries for System A: A.1_SIGNAL_TRIGGER, A.2_SIGNAL_VALIDATION, A.3_CONFLICT_MANAGEMENT, A.4_BRIDGE_EXPORT, A.5_FEEDBACK_MONITOR
Entries for System B: B_MQL4.1_SOCKET_BRIDGE, B_MQL4.2_FASTAPI_BRIDGE, B_MQL4.3_CSV_JSON_TRANSPORT, B_MQL4.4_LEGACY_PROFILES
Entries for System D: D_MQL4.1_ORDERSEND_WRAPPER, D_MQL4.2_RESPONSE_CLASSIFICATION, D_MQL4.3_RESULT_WRITING, D_MQL4.4_ERROR_HANDLING
All EA/MQL4 work cells list mt4_authoritative_reference_for_ai.json as required_reference_document

invariants: MQL4 work cells must not be confused with Python-side B1/B2/B3 work cells — use distinct IDs.
postconditions: All 13 EA-side work cell entries present in catalog.
No ID collision with B1/B2/B3 Python-side entries.

failure_modes.meaning: An EA-side work cell ID collides with an existing Python-side entry.
failure_modes.remediation: Use the B_MQL4.* prefix for MQL4 System B entries to distinguish from Python B1/B2/B3.

STEP-006.purpose: Author Risk Engine work cells (R1 through R9: position limits, portfolio exposure, margin, correlation, spread/slippage, circuit breakers, idempotency, sizing engine, decision assembler).

content_requirements: Entries for: R1_POSITION_LIMIT, R2_PORTFOLIO_EXPOSURE, R3_MARGIN_CHECK, R4_CORRELATION_GUARD, R5_SPREAD_SLIPPAGE_GUARD, R6_CIRCUIT_BREAKER, R7_IDEMPOTENCY_DEDUP, R8_SIZING_ENGINE, R9_RISK_DECISION_ASSEMBLER
R8_SIZING_ENGINE must be marked as deterministic (validation_gates must include a determinism check)
All risk work cells must declare forbidden_dependencies excluding calendar and UI namespaces

postconditions: All 9 risk engine work cell entries present in catalog.

failure_modes.meaning: R8_SIZING_ENGINE work cell does not include a determinism validation gate.
failure_modes.remediation: Add a validation gate asserting deterministic sizing output for identical inputs.

STEP-007.purpose: Author Signal Engine work cells (S1 ECO_HIGH/ECO_MED, S2 anticipation 1h/8h, S3 equity open, S4 all-indicator, S5 confidence scoring, S6 suppression reason mapping, S7 signal validation).

content_requirements: Entries for: S1_ECO_HIGH_MED, S2_ANTICIPATION_1H, S2_ANTICIPATION_8H, S3_EQUITY_OPEN, S4_ALL_INDICATOR, S5_CONFIDENCE_SCORING, S6_SUPPRESSION_MAPPING, S7_SIGNAL_VALIDATION
S1.*, S2.* entries must reference EAFIX CALENDAR DES RULES.pdf in required_reference_documents

postconditions: All 8 signal engine work cell entries present in catalog.

failure_modes.meaning: S1.* or S2.* work cell missing calendar rules reference.
failure_modes.remediation: Add EAFIX CALENDAR DES RULES.pdf to required_reference_documents.

STEP-008.purpose: Author Reentry Engine work cells (E1_OUTCOME_BUCKETIZER, E2_PROXIMITY_EVALUATOR, E3_MATRIX_LOOKUP, E4_REENTRY_INTENT_BUILDER).

content_requirements: Entries for: E1_OUTCOME_BUCKETIZER, E2_PROXIMITY_EVALUATOR, E3_MATRIX_LOOKUP, E4_REENTRY_INTENT_BUILDER
E3_MATRIX_LOOKUP must declare the reentry matrix version-pinning mechanism in its validation_gates
E2_PROXIMITY_EVALUATOR must list EAFIX CALENDAR DES RULES.pdf in required_reference_documents (calendar conflict guard)

postconditions: All 4 reentry engine work cell entries present in catalog.

failure_modes.meaning: E3_MATRIX_LOOKUP does not specify how the matrix version is pinned.
failure_modes.remediation: Add a validation gate: matrix version must be pinned at load time; mismatched version must raise an error.

STEP-009.purpose: Author UI Decomposition work cells (12 screen work cells + gateway endpoint work cells) by consulting CA-005.

content_requirements: Entries for screens: UI_LIVE_TRADING, UI_SIGNALS_HISTORY, UI_CONFIGURATION, UI_SYSTEM_STATUS, UI_TRADE_HISTORY, UI_HISTORY_ANALYTICS, UI_DDE_PRICE_FEED, UI_ECONOMIC_CALENDAR, UI_REENTRY_MATRIX, UI_REENTRY_PARAMETER_SETS, UI_ALERTS, UI_EQUITY_RISK
Entries for gateways matching ui_catalog.json REST and WebSocket endpoints
Screen names must match ui_catalog.json exactly — no renaming
Each UI work cell must declare its corresponding gateway endpoint in output_contracts

invariants: Screen names and endpoint IDs must be copied verbatim from ui_catalog.json, not inferred.
postconditions: All UI screen and gateway work cells present in catalog.
All screen names match ui_catalog.json exactly.

failure_modes.meaning: Work cell ID or screen name does not match ui_catalog.json.
failure_modes.remediation: Correct the name. Derive from ui_catalog.json only — do not invent screen names.

STEP-010.purpose: Run full schema validation against submodule_context_catalog.json and validate all parent_module values resolve against module_catalog.json.

content_requirements: Lists every work cell entry
Reports schema validation result per entry
Reports parent_module resolution result per entry
Reports forbidden_dependencies completeness per entry
Reports required_reference_documents completeness per entry
Overall pass/fail result

invariants: Catalog is read-only during this step.
preconditions: STEP-009 completed. All work cell entries authored.
postconditions: Validation report shows 0 failures.
GATE-SCHEMA-001, GATE-CELL-001, GATE-DEPS-001, GATE-DOCS-001 all pass.

failure_modes.meaning: One or more work cells fail schema or parent_module validation.
failure_modes.remediation: Return to the authoring step for the failing module and correct the entry. Re-run STEP-010.

STEP-011.purpose: Verify that the routing instructions document (CA-010) is consistent with the work cells now in the catalog. Identify any routing instruction entries that reference module IDs not present in the catalog, or vice versa.

content_requirements: Lists routing instructions that reference work cells present in the catalog
Lists routing instructions with no matching catalog entry (gap list)
Lists catalog entries with no routing instruction coverage (orphan list)
Recommendations for Phase 4 (routing rules) based on gaps and orphans

invariants: CA-010 is read-only. This step produces a report, not edits.
postconditions: Routing consistency report written. Any gaps or orphans documented for Phase 4 input.

failure_modes.meaning: A routing instruction references a module with no corresponding work cell in the catalog.
failure_modes.remediation: Return to PH-01 and author the missing work cells before closing Phase 1.

STEP-001.purpose: Design and write context_packet_schema.json — the JSON Schema governing the structure of every module context packet and work cell context file. Ensures consistency and enables GATE-PKT-001.

content_requirements: module context packet requires: module_id, parent_catalog_ref, source_files (array), contracts (object with input/output arrays), test_files (array), allowed_files (array), forbidden_files (array), required_reference_documents (array, min 1), acceptance_gates (array, min 1), ai_readme_path (string)
work cell context file requires: work_cell_id, parent_module_id, allowed_files (array), forbidden_files (array), required_reference_documents (array, min 1), acceptance_gates (array, min 1)
Schema is valid JSON Schema draft-07 or later

postconditions: context_packet_schema.json validates as valid JSON Schema.

failure_modes.meaning: Schema is not valid JSON Schema.
failure_modes.remediation: Run jsonschema meta-validation and correct errors.

STEP-002.purpose: Generate context packets for Calendar Pipeline modules (D2, D3, F2, D4). Each packet lists source_files verified against file_module_mapping.csv, contracts from the catalog, and required_reference_documents including CA-006.

content_requirements: context_packets/D2_CALENDAR_SOURCE_ADAPTER/context_packet.json
context_packets/D2_CALENDAR_SOURCE_ADAPTER/ai_readme.md
context_packets/D2_CALENDAR_SOURCE_ADAPTER/{work_cell_id}/work_cell_context.json for all D2.* work cells
Same pattern for D3, F2, D4
All source_files in packets must appear in file_module_mapping.csv for that module
All calendar packets list 'EAFIX CALENDAR DES RULES.pdf' in required_reference_documents
All packets pass context_packet_schema.json validation

invariants: source_files lists must be derived from file_module_mapping.csv only — no guessing.
postconditions: 4 module context packet directories created under context_packets/.
GATE-PKT-001 passes for all 4 modules.
GATE-STALE-001 passes (no file reference outside file_module_mapping.csv).

failure_modes.meaning: A context packet source_files entry is not present in file_module_mapping.csv for that module.
failure_modes.remediation: Remove the file from the packet. Only files in the mapping are allowed. If the mapping is wrong, that is a Phase 3 concern, not a Phase 2 fix.

failure_modes.meaning: A generated packet fails context_packet_schema.json validation.
failure_modes.remediation: Correct the packet fields to match the schema.

STEP-003.purpose: Generate context packets for MT4 Bridge modules (B1, B2, B3) using CA-004, CA-007, CA-008.

content_requirements: context_packets/B1_MT4_ADAPTER_TRANSPORT/context_packet.json and ai_readme.md
context_packets/B2_MT4_EA_EXECUTOR/context_packet.json and ai_readme.md
context_packets/B3_EXECUTION_EVENT_NORMALIZER/context_packet.json and ai_readme.md
All B2 packets list mt4_authoritative_reference_for_ai.json in required_reference_documents
All B1 packets list communication_channels.json in required_reference_documents

postconditions: 3 MT4 bridge module context packet directories created.
GATE-PKT-001 and GATE-STALE-001 pass.

failure_modes.meaning: Source file not in file_module_mapping.csv for the module.
failure_modes.remediation: Remove from packet. Do not add to mapping in Phase 2.

STEP-004.purpose: Generate context packets for all remaining modules: EA-Side MQL4 (A, B_MQL4, D_MQL4), Risk Engine (R1-R9), Signal Engine (S1-S7), Reentry Engine (E1-E4), and UI (all screens + gateways).

content_requirements: Packets for all EA-side, Risk, Signal, Reentry, and UI modules
UI packet screen IDs must match ui_catalog.json exactly
All packets pass context_packet_schema.json validation
All source_files verified against file_module_mapping.csv

postconditions: All module context packet directories created.
GATE-PKT-001 and GATE-STALE-001 pass for all modules.

failure_modes.meaning: Source file not in mapping for the module.
failure_modes.remediation: Remove from packet.

failure_modes.meaning: UI context packet ID does not match ui_catalog.json.
failure_modes.remediation: Correct to match ui_catalog.json exactly.

STEP-005.purpose: Run full validation across all context packets: schema check, file-in-mapping check, no-override check. Produce final validation report.

content_requirements: Lists all context_packet.json files found
Reports schema validation result per packet
Reports file-in-mapping result per packet
Reports whether any AUTHORITATIVE artifact was modified
Overall pass/fail

postconditions: Report shows 0 failures across all gates.

failure_modes.meaning: One or more packets fail validation.
failure_modes.remediation: Return to the authoring step for the failing module and correct. Re-run STEP-005.

STEP-006.purpose: Final non-override verification: confirm no AUTHORITATIVE context artifact (CA-001 through CA-009) has a modified timestamp or changed hash compared to STEP-001 baseline.

content_requirements: For each CA-001 through CA-009: current sha256 vs baseline sha256
PASS if all hashes match; FAIL if any differ
If FAIL: identify which artifact changed and stop — do not proceed to Phase 3

invariants: This step must not modify any file.
postconditions: GATE-NOOVERRIDE-001 passes. All AUTHORITATIVE artifact hashes match PH-01 baseline.

failure_modes.meaning: A canonical source artifact hash differs from Phase 1 baseline. Something modified it.
failure_modes.remediation: Halt all Phase 2 work. Restore the artifact from backup. Identify how the modification occurred and prevent recurrence. The entire catalog may need re-derivation.

validation_gates.purpose: All AUTHORITATIVE and CONSTRAINING context artifacts exist on disk and are readable.
validation_gates.command: python -c "import json, os, sys; log=json.load(open('.state/evidence/PH-01/STEP-001/context_artifact_verification.json')); failures=[a for a in log['artifacts'] if not a['exists'] or not a['readable']]; sys.exit(1 if failures else 0)"
validation_gates.failure_action: halt — do not begin catalog authoring until all artifacts are confirmed present

validation_gates.purpose: Every entry in submodule_context_catalog.json validates against submodule_catalog_schema.json.
validation_gates.failure_action: return to authoring step for failing module and correct

validation_gates.purpose: Every work cell has parent_module resolving to a module ID present in module_catalog.json.
validation_gates.command: python -c "import json, sys; cat=json.load(open('submodule_context_catalog.json')); mods={m['module_id'] for m in json.load(open('module_catalog.json'))['modules']}; failures=[c['work_cell_id'] for c in cat['work_cells'] if c['parent_module'] not in mods]; print(failures); sys.exit(1 if failures else 0)"
validation_gates.failure_action: correct parent_module values for failing entries

validation_gates.purpose: Every work cell declares a non-empty forbidden_dependencies list.
validation_gates.command: python -c "import json, sys; cat=json.load(open('submodule_context_catalog.json')); failures=[c['work_cell_id'] for c in cat['work_cells'] if not c.get('forbidden_dependencies')]; print(failures); sys.exit(1 if failures else 0)"
validation_gates.failure_action: add forbidden_dependencies to all failing work cells

validation_gates.purpose: Every work cell lists at least one required_reference_document.
validation_gates.command: python -c "import json, sys; cat=json.load(open('submodule_context_catalog.json')); failures=[c['work_cell_id'] for c in cat['work_cells'] if not c.get('required_reference_documents')]; print(failures); sys.exit(1 if failures else 0)"
validation_gates.failure_action: add at least one required_reference_document to each failing work cell

validation_gates.purpose: No routing instruction in CA-010 references a module with zero work cells in the catalog.
validation_gates.command: python -c "import json, sys; report=json.load(open('.state/evidence/PH-01/STEP-011/routing_consistency_report.json')); failures=report.get('critical_gaps',[]); sys.exit(1 if failures else 0)"
validation_gates.failure_action: author missing work cells before closing Phase 1

validation_gates.purpose: context_packet_schema.json is a valid JSON Schema.
Every context_packet.json validates against context_packet_schema.json AND every source_file in each packet is present in file_module_mapping.csv for that module.
validation_gates.command_note: This script does not yet exist. Author it as part of PH-02/STEP-005 or treat as NOT_AUTOMATABLE with manual file-by-file review.
validation_gates.failure_action: remove invalid file references; correct schema violations

validation_gates.purpose: No context packet references a source file not present in file_module_mapping.csv.
validation_gates.command: python scripts/validate_context_packets.py context_packets/ context_packet_schema.json file_module_mapping.csv --check stale_only
validation_gates.failure_action: remove stale file references from packets

validation_gates.purpose: No AUTHORITATIVE context artifact (CA-001 through CA-009) was modified during Phases 1 or 2. Hash comparison against Phase 1 baseline.
validation_gates.command: python -c "import json, hashlib, sys; baseline=json.load(open('.state/evidence/PH-01/STEP-001/context_artifact_verification.json')); failures=[a for a in baseline['artifacts'] if hashlib.sha256(open(a['path'],'rb').read()).hexdigest()!=a['sha256']]; print(failures); sys.exit(1 if failures else 0)"
validation_gates.failure_action: HALT. Restore modified artifact from backup. Do not proceed to Phase 3.

anti_pattern_detection.tier_note: Triggers are scoped to the documentation/catalog work of Phases 1-2. Code-execution triggers from the full template (patternless_execution, unregistered_executor_invocation, etc.) are not applicable here — those apply in Phases 3-4.

triggers.name: authoritative_artifact_modified
triggers.description: Any CA-001 through CA-009 artifact is written, edited, or removed during Phases 1 or 2.
triggers.action: halt — restore from backup, investigate, restart affected steps

triggers.name: undeclared_file_mutation
triggers.description: A file is created or modified that does not appear in artifact_intent_manifest.
triggers.detection: File diff at end of step reveals mutations outside declared AINT-* records
triggers.action: halt — either add the file to artifact_intent_manifest (if intentional) or delete the undeclared file

triggers.name: work_cell_without_parent_module
triggers.description: A work cell entry in the catalog has a parent_module value that does not resolve in module_catalog.json.

triggers.name: work_cell_without_forbidden_dependencies
triggers.description: A work cell has an empty or missing forbidden_dependencies list.
triggers.action: halt — declare forbidden_dependencies before the catalog is used for any AI context routing

triggers.name: work_cell_without_required_reference_document
triggers.description: A work cell has no required_reference_documents entry.
triggers.action: halt — an AI agent using this work cell would have no canonical reference to consult

triggers.name: stale_file_reference_in_packet
triggers.description: A context packet source_files entry is not present in file_module_mapping.csv for that module.
triggers.action: halt — remove the stale reference; do not fix the mapping in Phase 2

triggers.name: screen_name_invented
triggers.description: A UI work cell ID or screen name was invented rather than copied from ui_catalog.json.
triggers.detection: Manual comparison of UI work cell IDs against ui_catalog.json; or GATE-CELL-001 structural check
triggers.action: halt — correct to match ui_catalog.json exactly

triggers.name: context_packet_contradicts_canonical
triggers.description: A context packet ai_readme.md or context_packet.json contains a statement that contradicts an AUTHORITATIVE artifact (e.g., a different list of OrderSend parameters than mt4_authoritative_reference_for_ai.json).
triggers.detection: Manual review; no automated gate exists for semantic contradiction in Phase 2
triggers.action: halt — correct packet content to match the AUTHORITATIVE artifact; the packet is derived from it, not the other way around

triggers.name: routing_gap_ignored
triggers.description: Phase 1 STEP-011 produced a critical_gaps report entry but it was not resolved before closing Phase 1.
triggers.action: halt — author the missing work cells before proceeding to Phase 2

triggers.name: work_cell_id_collision
triggers.description: Two work cells have the same work_cell_id, including Python-side vs MQL4-side entries.
triggers.detection: GATE-SCHEMA-001 catches duplicates if schema enforces uniqueness; also detectable by script
triggers.action: halt — rename one of the colliding entries using a unique prefix convention

actions.critical: halt — do not proceed to the next step or phase

staleness_policy.description: Defines what triggers re-derivation of the catalog and context packets when canonical sources change.

trigger_conditions.trigger: Any module added, removed, or renamed requires re-derivation of all work cell parent_module references and all context packet module_id values.
Any file added or removed from a module requires re-derivation of the affected module's context packet source_files and allowed_files lists.
Any screen or endpoint added, removed, or renamed requires re-derivation of all UI work cells.
Any rule change requires manual review of all D2, D3, D4, S1, S2, E2 work cells and their context packets for consistency.
Any transport change requires review of B1 and B2 work cells.
Any change requires review of all B2 and EA-side work cells.

staleness_policy.re_derivation_gate: GATE-CTX-001 must be re-run after any canonical source change before catalog use continues.
staleness_policy.note: Phases 3 and 4 must not begin until a clean GATE-NOOVERRIDE-001 and GATE-CTX-001 pass on the current state.

phase_3_4_prerequisites.description: Conditions that must be true before Phases 3 (source refactoring) and 4 (routing rules) begin. These are NOT part of this plan; they are entry gates for the next plan document (to be authored under full newPhasePlanProcess v3.3 template).
required_conditions: submodule_context_catalog.json passes GATE-SCHEMA-001, GATE-CELL-001, GATE-DEPS-001, GATE-DOCS-001.
All context packets pass GATE-PKT-001 and GATE-STALE-001.
PATTERN_INDEX.yaml bootstrapped with at minimum: PAT-FILE-REFACTOR and PAT-ROUTING-RULE patterns.
At least one registered executor (EXEC-*) exists for each pattern type.
Phase 3-4 plan document authored under full newPhasePlanProcess v3.3 template.
Phase 3-4 plan references this document (decomposition_plan_ph1_ph2.json) as CA-DERIVED-001 in its referenced_context_artifacts.
